operationalSituations.description: Developer imports RELAY sub-packages (can, lin, rcp, …) to build x-Net adapters or relay.Node wrappers — canonical types and validators used in production code paths
CI pipeline runs relay conform as a release gate — the binary's capabilities document and sentinel errors are verified against the spec before the x-Net release is tagged
Auditor uses the RELAY spec (spec/relay-spec.md) and conformance report as the authoritative reference when certifying an x-Net implementation for use in a safety-critical vehicle system

hazards.description: ValidateFrame accepts an invalid frame — a structurally invalid CAN or LIN frame passes validation and reaches the bus
hazards.source: Missing or incorrect constraint check in ValidateFrame (e.g. RTR+FD, BRS without FD, ID out of range, data length exceeded)

hazards.description: ToMessage / FromMessage loses mandatory fields — relay.Node routes a message missing a protocol-essential field, causing the receiving node to act on incomplete data
hazards.source: Missing field copy, off-by-one in Meta map, or incorrect strconv conversion

hazards.description: Error sentinels not distinct — errors.Is(err, relay.ErrClosed) returns true for a wrong sentinel (e.g. ErrNotConnected), causing an application to misdiagnose a connection failure
hazards.source: Sentinel values accidentally equal, or incorrect %w wrapping in protocol-specific errors

hazards.description: MatchTopic wildcard error — a subscription receives messages it should not (false positive) or misses messages it should receive (false negative)
hazards.source: Incorrect MQTT §4.7 wildcard implementation, '$' prefix exemption missing, or '#' parent-level match wrong

hazards.description: Spec defect — RELAY spec §15 or §8 contains an incorrect field mapping or constraint, causing all conformant x-Net implementations to implement the same wrong behaviour
hazards.source: Editorial error in spec or incorrect derivation from protocol standards

hazards.description: Conformance false positive — relay conform reports PASS for an x-Net implementation that violates a mandatory requirement, allowing a non-conformant implementation to be released
hazards.source: Incomplete conformance checks, schema mismatch not caught, or exit-code logic error in relay conform

safetyGoals.description: RELAY shall implement all §15.1 and §15.3 ValidateFrame constraints such that every structural violation results in ErrInvalidFrame and never passes validation
RELAY shall implement ToMessage / FromMessage as lossless round-trips for all mandatory fields specified in §15.7
safetyGoals.safeState: Field value preserved exactly; routing carries correct data
safetyGoals.fssrRef: REQ-RELAY-032 REQ-RELAY-034 REQ-RELAY-037 REQ-RELAY-039 REQ-RELAY-041 REQ-RELAY-043

safetyGoals.description: RELAY shall define the four error sentinels as mutually distinct non-nil errors such that errors.Is returns false for every cross-sentinel comparison
safetyGoals.safeState: Correct sentinel detected; application takes correct recovery action

safetyGoals.description: RELAY shall implement MatchTopic to exactly conform to MQTT §4.7 wildcard semantics including '$' prefix exemption and '#' parent-level match
The RELAY spec shall be version-controlled, reviewed before each release, and any correction to §15 or §8 shall require a MINOR version increment and CHANGELOG entry
safetyGoals.safeState: Spec defect found before x-Net implementations adopt the incorrect version

safetyGoals.description: relay conform shall exit non-zero whenever any mandatory conformance requirement fails; requirements 1–9 and 12 shall be machine-checked (§17)
safetyGoals.safeState: Non-conformant implementation not released; CI gate blocks tag
